title: T-051 — D365 posture contract: optional integration, external IDs only, no source-of-truth ownership
prompt:
# T-051 — D365 posture contract: optional integration, external IDs only, no source-of-truth ownership

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5, §11, §W0-v4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Create a Foundation D365 posture contract that prevents downstream tasks from treating D365 as the product/BOM source of truth or factory-release gate.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T0-docs`.

## Implementation contract
Create `_foundation/contracts/d365-posture.md`. Define D365 as optional import/export/integration posture only. Allowed Phase 1 capabilities: optional import/export jobs, capability registry entry, item/BOM/customer/supplier/location/UoM pull as external data, production confirmation/inventory/shipment/quality-hold push when policy-enabled. Forbidden: D365 as source of truth for FG, shared BOM, factory_spec, release approval, or factory usability. Export timing: no D365 export before Technical factory-use approval unless org policy explicitly enables non-usable preload/export, clearly marked not factory release. Include external_id mapping strategy and failure/DLQ expectations.

## Files in scope
- _foundation/contracts/d365-posture.md [create]

## Dependencies
- T-047
- T-048

## Cross-module dependencies
- 02-SETTINGS import/export capability registry; 03-TECHNICAL factory_spec approval

## Acceptance criteria
1. Contract says D365 is optional and never source of truth.
2. Contract separates D365 export/preload from factory release/factory usability.
3. Contract mentions backend jobs/capability registry, not UI shell only.
4. Contract uses `org_id` in all scoped examples.

## Test strategy
- Run grep checks for `never source of truth`, `factory-use approval`, `non-usable preload`, `capability registry`, `org_id`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement D365 adapter code.
- Do not permit D365 export to imply factory release.
- Do not use tenant_id as business scope.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
